subject: Cloud Computing
title: BigQuery and the Data Edge
description: A quick, practical guide to BigQuery and the Data Edge.

hook.text: Run a SQL query across a petabyte of data and get results in seconds. That's BigQuery.

buildup.text: Google built BigQuery on technology it uses internally to analyze billions of web pages daily.

discovery.text: It separates storage from compute, so you only pay for queries — not for keeping a cluster running.

twist.text: BigQuery is often the reason companies choose Google Cloud. It's a killer app that pulls in other services.

climax.text: In data analytics, Google Cloud genuinely leads. BigQuery set the standard competitors try to match.

punchline.text: BigQuery proves that one great product can anchor a whole cloud.

quiz.question: What makes BigQuery architecturally different from traditional databases?
options: It separates storage from compute, so you pay only for queries
It stores all data in your local browser for faster access
It requires a dedicated cluster running 24/7 to function
